rawCommits.subject: fix(agents): cascade-delete project bindings when deleting an agent
rawCommits.body:
The server's `delete_agent` returns a 409 ("still added to: Home")
whenever any `project_agent` row references the template, but the UI
never tore down those bindings on the user's behalf — leaving Delete
as a dead-end with no obvious path forward, especially for the
auto-created Home binding that the chat-side self-heal kept
resurrecting.

Centralize the workflow in `useCascadeDeleteAgent`, used by both the
agent detail panel and the sidebar context menu, so confirming Delete
removes every binding first and then deletes the template in one
action. The confirm modal now spells out which projects will be
detached. Server-side, `ensure_agent_home_project_and_binding` skips
the auto-bind when the template is already gone upstream so the
cascade can't race the chat heal.

Also surface why a Home binding may not appear under Home in the
Projects sidebar by hinting "(in another org)" / "(archived)" next to
each row in the agent's Projects tab.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: cascade-delete project bindings when deleting an agent

rawCommits.subject: refactor: phase 5 - split three largest files (>500 lines)
rawCommits.body:
- PermissionsTab.tsx (~1043 lines) -> PermissionsTab/ folder with
  permissions-utils (pure helpers), permissions-rows (sub-components +
  AutosaveStatus + ActiveHarnessToolsSection), usePermissionsForm
  (draft state + setters + memoised derived), usePermissionsAutosave
  (debounced PUT + status/badge timers + flush-on-unmount). Orchestrator
  stays under 280 lines; existing test moved into the folder via git mv
  with relative-import path updates only.
- aura-events.ts (~842 lines) -> aura-events/ folder split into
  payloads.ts (PhaseTimingEntry + LoopId/Activity + Sender +
  ChatAttachment + isLoopActivityActive), event-types.ts (EventType
  const + AuraEventBase + assembled AuraEvent union), events-domain.ts
  (Message/Streaming/Agent/Spec/Task/Loop variants), events-system.ts
  (Build/Test/Git/Harness/Billing/Process/Generation/Other variants),
  parsers.ts (parseAuraEvent + isValidEventType + AuraEventOfType +
  AuraEventContent), and an index barrel. Original .ts deleted; existing
  imports continue to resolve via folder/index.
- notes-store.ts (~632 lines) -> stores/notes/ folder with notes-utils
  (shared helpers + types + autosave debounce), notes-tree-slice (CRUD),
  notes-content-slice (autosave + flushNote rename rewiring),
  notes-comments-slice, notes-sidekick-slice (active-note pointer +
  reveal-in-folder), notes-store.ts (Zustand composer + selector hooks)
  and an index barrel. Original interface/src/stores/notes-store.ts kept
  as a one-line re-export so call sites are untouched.
- No behavior changes; existing tests pass without modification (paths
  preserved or re-exported; only test edits are import-path bumps in the
  moved PermissionsTab.test.tsx).

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: phase 5 - split three largest files (>500 lines)

rawCommits.subject: test(theme): phase 6 - validation tests, smoke checklist, and checkpoint summary
rawCommits.body:
- Add SettingsView smoke test asserting AppearanceSection renders alongside the About panel.
- Strengthen AppearanceSection.test.tsx with an explicit assertion that toggling theme persists to the ZUI localStorage entry (theme=light is JSON-serialized into the same blob as accent).
- Restore the lint baseline after Phase 5 introduced +1 warning: usePermissionsAutosave.ts now has a targeted react-hooks/exhaustive-deps suppression on the unmount-flush ref read (intentional latest-value behavior), and a now-unused react-hooks/set-state-in-effect disable directive is removed.
- New interface/docs/theme-smoke-checklist.md walks the user through manual verification of phases 1-5 (theme switching, accent, surface coverage, mobile, regression checks).
- New interface/docs/theme-checkpoint-phase-6.md summarises the checkpoint state, commit history, automated-gate results (tsc clean, lint delta 0 vs baseline, scoped tests pass, build succeeds), pre-existing failures, and a Phase 7-14 next-steps pointer.

Verified gates: npx tsc -b clean, npm run lint = 219 errors / 34 warnings (matches Phase 1 baseline), scoped vitest run for SettingsView/PermissionsTab/notes/AppearanceSection 14 files / 61 tests pass, npm run build successful in ~2.3s with both highlight.js light+dark CSS chunks emitted.
rawCommits.cleanSubject: phase 6 - validation tests, smoke checklist, and checkpoint summary

rawCommits.subject: fix(desktop): keep window controls accessible on boot/error overlay
rawCommits.body:
Adds a minimal native-HTML fallback titlebar in interface/index.html that
sits above the splash and boot-error overlay, so the frameless tao window
stays draggable and closable even when /src/main.tsx never mounts (e.g.
dev server unreachable, Vite import error). The fallback uses the same
window.ipc bridge wry exposes for the React WindowControls component
(minimize/maximize/close/drag) and is hidden via html.aura-app-ready
once React's DesktopTitlebar takes over. Mirrors native-titlebar-drag.ts
so the JS pointerdown drag fallback only fires on macOS/Linux where
-webkit-app-region: drag is ignored by WKWebView/WebKitGTK.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: keep window controls accessible on boot/error overlay

rawCommits.subject: refactor(chat): phase 13 - extract buildUserChatMessage shared helper
rawCommits.body:
Per the chat-streaming architecture mini-doc (interface/docs/chat-streaming.md),
full consolidation of the chat-streaming hook cluster was rejected because
every EventType branch carries project-specific side effects in the
useChatStream variant that the standalone-agent variant deliberately
omits. A discriminated-union wrapper would be a thin facade with no real
consolidation value.

The one piece of genuine duplication is the optimistic user-message
construction in sendMessage. Both useChatStream and useAgentChatStream
build the same { id: temp-..., role: "user", content, contentBlocks }
shape with the same trimmed || fallback || attachment-label fallback
chain. Extract this into buildUserChatMessage in attachment-helpers.ts
so the temp-id convention and fallback chain live in one place.

No behaviour change. Existing useChatStream and useAgentChatStream
test suites assert on entry.events[0].role and entry.events[0].content
and continue to pass; new tests added for the helper itself.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: phase 13 - extract buildUserChatMessage shared helper

rawCommits.subject: fix(a11y): phase 14 - restore keyboard focus visibility across the app
rawCommits.body:
- Remove global *, *:focus, *:focus-visible { outline: none !important; } from interface/src/index.css that suppressed all focus indicators since Phase 1.
- Add a single :focus-visible base rule (2px outline, --color-focus-ring, 2px offset, --radius-sm) so keyboard focus is visible everywhere; mouse clicks don't trigger focus-visible per modern browser semantics.
- Per-element overrides only on #root and .titlebar-drag, which can receive programmatic focus but are not really keyboard-interactive surfaces; everything else falls through to the global :focus-visible rule. Existing per-component outline:none rules in CSS modules are intentional design choices (e.g. ChatInputBar textarea, sidekick tab bar, AppNavRail taskbar buttons) and are left alone; they all sit at higher specificity or with !important and continue to win.
- Token values (--color-focus-ring: #ffffff dark / #111827 light) come from vendor/zui themes and are not changed in this phase.
- Update interface/docs/theme-smoke-checklist.md (rename to "Phases 1-14") with a Keyboard / a11y section listing the manual tab-through checks.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: phase 14 - restore keyboard focus visibility across the app

rawCommits.subject: feat(theme): default Panel variant to solid so panels match the taskbar bg
rawCommits.body:
ZUI Panel previously defaulted to variant="glass" (rgba(9,9,11,0.15)),
which rendered as a barely-visible overlay against the page bg. The
taskbar pill chrome resolves to color-mix(var(--color-bg) 90%, transparent) -
essentially var(--color-bg). Switching the default variant to "solid"
(which uses var(--color-bg)) makes any unannotated <Panel /> instance
read as the same surface tone as the taskbar pill in dark mode (and
mirror-light in light mode).

Every existing in-tree Panel call site already specifies variant
explicitly (most use variant="solid"), so this default change has no
visible regression risk for current consumers but anchors a more
sensible default for future Panel usage.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: default Panel variant to solid so panels match the taskbar bg

rawCommits.subject: feat(settings): merge app settings (Appearance + others) into OrgSettingsPanel
rawCommits.body:
The taskbar gear icon previously opened OrgSettingsPanel ("Team Settings"),
which only contained team-scoped sections (General, Members, Invites,
Rewards, Billing, Z Credit History, Privacy, Integrations). The
appearance/theme controls lived in a separate SettingsView at
/projects/settings/appearance with no UI affordance from the desktop
chrome to reach it. Result: users couldn't find theme controls without
typing the URL.

This consolidates both surfaces into the single modal opened by the
taskbar gear:

- Renames the modal title from "Team Settings" to "Settings" and the
  taskbar gear's title/aria-label from "Team settings" to "Settings".
- Splits the sidebar Navigator into two grouped sections separated by
  small uppercase labels ("APP" / "TEAM"), with App listed first so
  Appearance is immediately visible.
- App-scoped sections (Appearance, Notifications, Keyboard, About,
  Advanced) reuse the existing components from views/SettingsView/* so
  they stay in sync with the desktop deep-link route and the mobile
  settings screen.
- App sections render even when no active org is loaded; the
  "Team settings are currently unavailable" message now only blocks
  team-scoped sections, not the entire modal.
- Section type lives in useOrgSettingsData and is exported so the panel
  can type-narrow against an isOrgSection() guard.

The standalone SettingsView at /projects/settings/:section continues to
exist (still used by the mobile shell and remains valid for deep links).
This change is purely additive on the desktop side.

Tests:
- Adds OrgSettingsPanel cases for app nav items, switching to Appearance,
  the renamed modal title, and Appearance still working when no org.
- Updates the BottomTaskbar test to look for the renamed "Settings"
  button. The pre-existing render-churn test failure (LayoutGrid not in
  its lucide-react mock) is unchanged and out of scope.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: merge app settings (Appearance + others) into OrgSettingsPanel

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): pin active history key in LRU to stop CEO chat blink
rawCommits.body:
Background sidebar prefetches were evicting the currently-displayed
chat panel's entry from chat-history-store's LRU (capped at
MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES = 8). The CEO/SuperAgent chat is the most common
victim because it sits at index 0 (typically opened first), so the
9th sidebar prefetch dropped it. The renderer then snapped to the
empty state until the next refetch repopulated the entry -- the
"CEO chat blink" failure mode previously masked (but not eliminated)
by the lastNonEmptyRef guard in use-conversation-snapshot.ts. A
re-render burst from the new theme toggle made the race fire often
enough to be reproducible from a single click.

Root-cause fix: add pinnedKeys: Set<string> to chat-history-store
plus pinKey / unpinKey actions, and teach withBoundedHistoryEntry
to skip pinned keys during eviction (with a defensive fallback for
the impossible all-keys-pinned case). useChatHistorySync pins the
active historyKey on mount and unpins on unmount, so the key
backing the currently-rendered chat panel can never be the eviction
victim of a background prefetch. Coverage:
  - 4 new tests in chat-history-store.test.ts (pin survives 10
    prefetches, unpin restores eviction, pinKey idempotent,
    unpinKey no-op for unknown keys)
  - 2 new tests in use-chat-history-sync.test.ts (pin on mount /
    unpin on unmount lifecycle, undefined historyKey skips pin)

The lastNonEmptyRef guard is intentionally retained as defence in
depth for legitimately-transient mid-turn empty frames during
streaming, which are independent of LRU eviction.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: pin active history key in LRU to stop CEO chat blink

rawCommits.subject: feat(theme): flatten dark side panels to taskbar background
rawCommits.body:
Defaults --color-sidebar-bg and --color-sidekick-bg to var(--color-bg)
in dark mode so the left sidebar, right sidekick, and taskbar pills all
read as the same shade. Previously sidebar resolved to --color-surface
(#0e0e10) and sidekick to --color-elevated (#131316), producing a
visible vertical stripe against the titlebar/taskbar at --color-bg
(#09090b). Light mode is unchanged. CustomTokensPanel overrides still
win via document.documentElement.style.setProperty, so anyone who
preferred the previous separation can restore it.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: flatten dark side panels to taskbar background

rawCommits.subject: style(titlebar): render sidekick drawer toggle in primary text color when open
rawCommits.body:
The PanelRight icon in WindowControls used ZUI Button's selected state,
which paints the icon in --color-accent with a glow. Visually this read
as an in-app accent emphasis instead of a window-chrome control.

Override the selected color for just this button via a class+attribute
selector on aria-pressed="true" (specificity 0,2,0 beats ZUI's
.selected at 0,1,0 without !important). Uses --color-text-primary so
the icon stays theme-aware (white in dark mode, dark text in light).
Every other selected ZUI Button across the app keeps its accent
treatment.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style(titlebar): render sidekick drawer toggle in primary text color when open

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): reveal cold-load transcript proactively to stop CEO black panel
rawCommits.body:
ChatPanel's cold-load reveal relied solely on ChatMessageList's one-shot
`onInitialAnchorReady` callback, which is gated by an `initialLayoutReadyKeyRef`
keyed by streamKey and fires only ONCE per (streamKey, hasMessages=true)
pair. When re-entering CEO/MEOW after the chat-history-store evicted the
events entry (MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES=8) while the message-store thread for the
same streamKey carried over from the prior visit, the child fired the gate
immediately on first commit (messages.length>0) but `handleInitialAnchorReady`
bailed because historyResolved was still false. When history finally arrived,
the child's gate stayed latched and never re-fired -- leaving
.messageContentHidden (visibility: hidden) permanently applied. The panel
rendered fully black even though sidebar previews kept showing the last
message correctly (previewLastMessages has its own 100-entry bound).

Fix: extend ChatPanel's existing [historyResolved, messages.length] reveal
effect with a third branch that schedules the same double-rAF reveal
directly when cold-load is active with both flags satisfied. The reveal
becomes robust to either ordering of the two signals; the existing
cancelAnimationFrame guard prevents the child callback path and the new
effect path from double-scheduling.

Tests:
- New regression covering the carry-over + late historyResolved race.
- Updated "re-arms cold-load when the user actually switches chats" and
  renamed/flipped "keeps cold-load history hidden until the initial anchor
  is ready"; both were locking in the buggy "transcript stays hidden"
  surface, which the proactive reveal now correctly resolves.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: reveal cold-load transcript proactively to stop CEO black panel

rawCommits.subject: fix(theme): make light mode actually visible across chat, rails, sidekick, modals
rawCommits.body:
CSS modules across the app hardcoded `color: #fff` and `background: #000`,
and many referenced an undefined `--color-bg-secondary` token whose
`#1e1e1e` fallback fired in both themes. The result in light mode was
white-on-white text, white-on-white icon hovers, and dark-slab panels
embedded in an otherwise white app shell.

This pass routes the user-visible surfaces (chat bubbles, ChatPanel,
LLMOutput, AppNavRail / Sidekick icon hovers, ProjectsNav, project list,
ActivityCard, modals, mobile screens) through the existing semantic
tokens (--color-text-primary, --color-bg, --color-surface,
--color-overlay-*, --color-border) so they flip with the theme.

Token layer changes (interface/src/styles/tokens.css):
- Define --color-bg-secondary, --color-text, --color-bg-hover as
  theme-aware aliases so the dozens of consumers using these legacy
  names stop falling back to a hardcoded dark hex.
- Bump light-mode --color-text-secondary (#374151) and
  --color-text-muted (#4b5563) to meet WCAG AA contrast on white.

Also invert the AURA wordmark PNG in light mode via a single
[data-theme='light'] img[data-aura-wordmark] { filter: invert(1); }
rule in index.css and tag the three render sites (DesktopTitlebar,
MobileTopbar, LoginView) with data-aura-wordmark. The asset is white
text on transparent, so inversion gives perfect black text without a
second PNG.

Extend check-module-css-colors.mjs to lock in the newly clean modules
(AppNavRail, Sidekick, ProjectsNav, ProjectsPlusButton, two mobile
screens) so future regressions are caught by lint:colors.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: make light mode actually visible across chat, rails, sidekick, modals

rawCommits.subject: feat(theme): make titlebar/topbar theme toggle a binary light <-> dark switch
rawCommits.body:
The titlebar and mobile-topbar theme buttons used to cycle through three
states (dark -> light -> system -> dark), but per product direction the
toggle should be a simple two-state switch. The system option is dropped
from the cycle and from the icon set.

cycleTheme(theme, resolvedTheme) now flips between dark and light. If the
caller is currently on `system` (e.g. via a previously stored preference
or programmatic setTheme), the next click resolves to the OPPOSITE of
whatever prefers-color-scheme is currently rendering, so the click always
produces a visible change instead of being a no-op for users sitting on
their OS-matched theme.

The icon and aria-label now mirror the resolved theme — sun for light,
moon for dark — even when the stored preference is `system`, so the UI
always tells the user what they're actually looking at.

Tests updated for the two-state cycle on both the desktop helper and the
MobileThemeToggleButton.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: make titlebar/topbar theme toggle a binary light <-> dark switch

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): keep user message bubble dark in light mode for sender contrast
rawCommits.body:
The previous light-mode contrast pass routed the user bubble background
through --color-bg-secondary (now an alias for --color-elevated), which
in light mode is white. The result was a near-invisible white bubble
sitting on the white page background — losing the sender/assistant
visual differentiation that the bubble exists to communicate.

Pin .bubbleUser to a dark background + white text in BOTH themes.
Other consumers of --color-bg-secondary keep their theme-aware behavior;
only this one rule needed to hold dark because its job is contrast
against the page, not against the surrounding chrome.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: keep user message bubble dark in light mode for sender contrast

rawCommits.subject: style(titlebar): unify theme/host icons with sidekick + window controls
rawCommits.body:
The titlebar action row mixed three button styles: the theme toggle used
size=16 icons without `rounded="md"`, the host-settings button matched
it, and the WindowControls inner row used size=14 strokeWidth=2 icons
with `rounded="md"` and `gap: 0`. The result was a visible step in icon
size and spacing right next to the sidekick toggle.

Align the theme toggle and host-settings buttons with the sidekick /
window-control button shape — same `size="sm" rounded="md" iconOnly`
Button, same 14px lucide icons with strokeWidth=2 — and switch the
host `.titleActions` container from `gap: var(--space-2)` to `gap: 0`
so every chrome icon sits on the same rhythm as the WindowControls
inner row.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style(titlebar): unify theme/host icons with sidekick + window controls

rawCommits.subject: Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into theming/phases-1-14
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into theming/phases-1-14

rawCommits.subject: feat(apps): hide Debug from taskbar by default
rawCommits.body:
Add a `defaultHidden` flag to the AuraApp registry and mark Debug
with it, so the Apps modal seeds Debug into the "Hidden" section on
first load instead of cluttering the visible taskbar strip. The
storage layer now distinguishes "no saved hidden-apps entry" (null,
falls back to the registry-derived defaults) from "user explicitly
cleared the list" (empty array, honored as-is), so dragging Debug
into Visible persists across reloads.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: hide Debug from taskbar by default

rawCommits.subject: fix(theme): make form inputs visible in light mode
rawCommits.body:
ZUI's Input, Search, Textarea, Select, PanelLogin provider buttons,
and the app-level FolderPickerField were rendering with white-on-
white chrome on light theme: hard-coded `rgba(255, 255, 255, *)`
backgrounds/borders disappeared on `#ffffff` modal surfaces and the
generic `--color-border` (`#e5e7eb`) was too soft to read as a
border on white.

Introduce dedicated `--color-input-{bg,bg-hover,bg-focus,border,
border-hover,border-focus,focus-shadow}` tokens in vendor/zui
`themes.css` so form inputs can be tuned independently from generic
surface tokens. Dark values keep the original subtle white-on-dark
pill look. Light values use `#ffffff` fills with a `#d1d5db`
(gray-300) border and `#9ca3af` / `#6b7280` hover/focus to give
form fields a familiar visible outline. All six components now
route through the new tokens.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: make form inputs visible in light mode

rawCommits.subject: feat(ui): align Settings, Aura3D, and Apps modal title bars with sidebar search
rawCommits.body:
Make the Settings page header, the Aura3D image/3D Model tab bar, and the
Apps modal header all 32px tall (control-height-sm) so they line up
horizontally with the sidebar search input.

- SettingsView: replace zui Page wrapper with a custom 32px title bar;
  move padding/scroll behavior into the local layout.
- Aura3DMainPanel: drop the tabBar's top padding and pass a tabClassName
  that pins each tab to control-height-sm (the inner Tabs already renders
  the bottom border).
- Apps modal: add an opt-in headerClassName prop to zui's Modal so we can
  shrink only this modal's header to control-height-sm without affecting
  every other modal app-wide.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: align Settings, Aura3D, and Apps modal title bars with sidebar search

rawCommits.subject: fix(theme): remove focus halo on Input
rawCommits.body:
Drop the `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--color-input-focus-shadow)`
that ZUI's `Input` painted on focus. Sibling input-shaped
components (`Search`, `Textarea`, `Select`) never had a focus
shadow, so this brings `Input` in line: focus is now indicated
purely by the darker `--color-input-border-focus` border, matching
the rest of the form chrome. The now-unused
`--color-input-focus-shadow` token is removed from both dark and
light blocks in `vendor/zui/src/styles/themes.css`.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: remove focus halo on Input

rawCommits.subject: feat(zui): add compactHeader prop to Modal
rawCommits.body:
Wire ZUI Modal up to the existing `.headerCompact` CSS class via a
new `compactHeader?: boolean` prop. The class (32px tall,
`var(--control-height-sm)` with reduced padding) was already
defined in `Modal.module.css` but had no prop to opt into it —
callers had to inject it through `headerClassName`. This makes the
slim-header variant a first-class API so AppsModal and the other
sidebar-aligned modals can drop their bespoke `headerClassName`
overrides.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: add compactHeader prop to Modal

rawCommits.subject: fix(ui): apply 32px compact header to Apps and Org Settings modals
rawCommits.body:
The previous attempt set the Apps modal's compact header through a local
headerClassName override on the Modal component, but the rule lived in
AppsModal.module.css and competed with zui's Modal.header padding (same
specificity, source-order-dependent), so the override didn't always win
in production builds. Move the compact-header style into zui's Modal
stylesheet (right after .header, guaranteeing the cascade) and expose it
through the new `compactHeader` boolean prop introduced earlier.

- vendor/zui Modal.module.css: add `.headerCompact` (height 32px, padding
  0 var(--space-3)) and a `.headerCompact .closeButton { padding: 0 }`
  override so the X stays centered inside the slim header.
- AppsModal: replace `headerClassName={styles.compactHeader}` with the
  new `compactHeader` prop and drop the now-unused local CSS.
- OrgSettingsPanel ("Settings" modal): opt into `compactHeader` so its
  title bar also lines up horizontally with the sidebar search input.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: apply 32px compact header to Apps and Org Settings modals

rawCommits.subject: refactor(zui): make slim modal header the default
rawCommits.body:
Bake the 32px (`control-height-sm`) header layout straight into
`.header` in `Modal.module.css` so every ZUI Modal aligns with the
sidebar search bar by default. The opt-in `compactHeader` prop and
the now-redundant `.headerCompact` CSS class are removed; the
`.closeButton` padding is dropped to 0 to fit the slim row. Update
the two callers (`OrgSettingsPanel`, `AppsModal`) to drop the
no-longer-needed `compactHeader` flag.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: make slim modal header the default

rawCommits.subject: feat(aura3d): auto-select last project, latest image, and matching model
rawCommits.body:
Make the Aura3D nav and store remember context across app open/close
so users don't land on an empty editor:

- Aura3DNav: when nothing is selected, hydrate the selection from
  `getLastProject()` if its id is still in the projects list,
  otherwise fall back to the most recently created project. Expand
  that node so its tree shows up unfolded on first paint.
- aura3d-store: persist `selectedProjectId` to localStorage via
  `setLastProject` whenever the user picks a project (cleared
  selections are not persisted).
- aura3d-store: `setActiveTab` now auto-selects the latest image
  (and its linked 3D model, if any) when switching to the image
  tab with nothing selected, and the latest model when switching
  to the 3D tab. An existing selection is never overwritten.
- aura3d-store: `loadProjectArtifacts` seeds default selections
  (latest image, linked or latest model) after a project's
  artifacts come back, so opening a project lands on its newest
  asset instead of an empty pane. Switching projects clears the
  per-project selection state so the new project's defaults can
  take effect.
- Tests: cover the new auto-select branches and the
  localStorage persistence of `setSelectedProjectId`.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: auto-select last project, latest image, and matching model

rawCommits.subject: refactor(ui): extract shared InputBarShell from ChatInputBar and aura3d PromptInput
rawCommits.body: Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: extract shared InputBarShell from ChatInputBar and aura3d PromptInput

rawCommits.subject: Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into theming/phases-1-14
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into theming/phases-1-14

rawCommits.subject: fix(onboarding): only complete create tasks when count exceeds initial defaults
rawCommits.cleanSubject: only complete create tasks when count exceeds initial defaults

rawCommits.subject: ci: pass Z_BILLING_API_KEY secret to desktop nightly build
rawCommits.cleanSubject: pass Z_BILLING_API_KEY secret to desktop nightly build

rawCommits.subject: fix(desktop): bake Z_BILLING_API_KEY into desktop binary via build.rs
rawCommits.cleanSubject: bake Z_BILLING_API_KEY into desktop binary via build.rs

rendered.title: Light mode lands, plus an onboarding checklist for new users
rendered.intro: Today's nightly is dominated by one large thread: Aura grew a real theming system, with a working light mode, a Settings → Appearance editor, custom token overrides, and named presets you can import and export. On top of that, a first-run onboarding flow now greets new users with a welcome modal, a five-step checklist, and friendlier empty states. Smaller polish hits the chat panel reliability, modal chrome alignment, the Aura3D editor's memory of where you left off, and a few release-pipeline secrets needed for the desktop nightly.

entries.title: Theming system, light mode, onboarding checklist, and chat reliability fixes
entries.summary: A long, multi-phase push delivered Aura's theming system end-to-end, shipped a first-run onboarding experience, fixed two distinct CEO/MEOW chat blanking bugs, and tightened agent deletion and desktop window-control behavior.

items.text: Introduced a full theming system: tokenized colors with light/dark variants, a dynamic highlight.js theme, a Settings → Appearance section with mode and accent pickers, a titlebar quick toggle, and matching coverage for the process canvas, xterm terminal, and dozens of module CSS files. The toggle was later simplified to a binary light↔dark switch in the titlebar and mobile topbar.

Added a customizable Appearance editor: per-token color overrides for sidebar, sidekick, titlebar, and chrome surfaces persist per-theme, plus named presets with built-in Aura Dark/Light, JSON import/export, and a CustomTokensPanel that gracefully grays out for read-only built-ins.

Made light mode actually usable: rewrote hard-coded #fff/#000 across chat bubbles, LLM output, nav rails, sidekick, project list, modals, and mobile screens to flow through theme tokens; bumped light-mode secondary/muted text to WCAG AA contrast; inverted the AURA wordmark in light mode; and pinned the user message bubble dark in both themes for sender contrast. ZUI form inputs (Input, Search, Textarea, Select, login provider buttons) gained dedicated tokens so they're readable on white surfaces, with the focus halo dropped for consistency.

Settings became a proper sectioned surface with /settings/:section deep links (About, Appearance, Notifications, Keyboard, Advanced) on desktop and a list-then-detail screen on mobile; the taskbar gear now opens a single "Settings" modal that groups APP and TEAM sections so theme controls are finally reachable from the chrome.

Shipped a first-run onboarding experience: a Zustand-backed store with a 2-step welcome modal, a portal-rendered checklist of five tasks (send a message, create a project, create an agent, generate an image, explore billing), a help button in the taskbar, and a watcher that auto-completes tasks against baseline counts so pre-existing defaults aren't miscounted. The empty chat now shows prompt suggestion chips and a pulsing input glow, and agent/task empty states got more directive copy.

Killed two distinct CEO/MEOW chat blanking races: pinned the active history key in the chat-history LRU so background sidebar prefetches can't evict the visible thread, and made ChatPanel's cold-load reveal proactive so the transcript can't stay permanently hidden when historyResolved arrives after the initial anchor signal.

Agent deletion is no longer a dead-end: a new useCascadeDeleteAgent hook tears down all project bindings before deleting the template, the confirm modal lists which projects will be detached, and the server-side Home auto-bind skips templates that are already gone so the cascade can't race the chat self-heal. The agent's Projects tab also annotates bindings with "(in another org)" / "(archived)".

Restored keyboard focus visibility everywhere by removing the global outline:none !important rule installed during the theming refactor and replacing it with a single :focus-visible base style; added a native-HTML fallback titlebar so window minimize/maximize/close stay reachable even when the React app fails to mount on the frameless desktop window.

Polished desktop chrome: the sidekick drawer toggle now renders in primary text color when open instead of accent glow, the theme/host icon row aligns with the sidekick and window controls at 14px/sm with zero gap, and dark side panels flatten to the taskbar background to remove the visible vertical stripe.

Hid the Debug app from the taskbar by default via a new defaultHidden flag in the AuraApp registry, while preserving "explicitly cleared the list" semantics so users who drag Debug back into Visible keep that state across reloads.

Broke up four oversized files (PermissionsTab ~1043 lines, aura-events ~842 lines, notes-store ~632 lines, entities.ts ~700 lines, AgentEnvironment ~510 lines, stream handlers tests ~1334 lines, use-chat-history-sync ~530 lines) into focused folders with no behavior change, and added a lint:colors guard that fails CI on raw hex/rgb literals in module CSS files on the migrated denylist.
Aligned modal chrome with the sidebar: Settings, Aura3D's tab bar, and the Apps modal now sit at a 32px control-height-sm header so they line up with the sidebar search input.
Defaulted REQUIRE_ZERO_PRO to false on the server and in desktop builds so unset environments don't gate features behind a Zero Pro check.

entries.title: Mixpanel token wired into desktop nightly builds
entries.summary: Release Infrastructure: the desktop nightly workflow now receives the Mixpanel write key so analytics aren't silently dropped from shipped builds.

items.text: Pass VITE_MIXPANEL_TOKEN through the desktop nightly GitHub Actions workflow so the bundled web build can emit telemetry.

entries.title: Slim 32px modal header is now the ZUI default
entries.summary: A short iteration moved the compact modal header from a per-call override into a ZUI prop, then made it the default once it stopped winning the cascade reliably.

items.text: Promoted the 32px modal title bar from an AppsModal-local CSS override to a first-class compactHeader prop on ZUI Modal, then collapsed that prop entirely by baking the slim layout straight into Modal.module.css so every modal aligns with the sidebar search by default. AppsModal and the Settings modal drop their bespoke flags as a result.

entries.title: Mixpanel token wired into mobile nightly builds
entries.summary: Release Infrastructure: the mobile nightly workflow now also receives the Mixpanel write key, matching the desktop change earlier in the day.

items.text: Pass VITE_MIXPANEL_TOKEN through the mobile nightly GitHub Actions workflow so iOS/Android builds emit telemetry consistent with desktop.

entries.title: Aura3D remembers context, and chat/PromptInput share one shell
entries.summary: Aura3D stops landing users on an empty editor by remembering project, image, and model selections, while ChatInputBar and the Aura3D PromptInput consolidate onto a single shared input shell.

items.text: Aura3D now hydrates the last selected project from localStorage, falls back to the most recently created project, and auto-selects the latest image (and its linked 3D model) or latest model when switching tabs, so opening the app lands on real content instead of a blank pane.
Extracted a shared InputBarShell (with ModelPicker) used by both the chat input bar and the Aura3D prompt input, deleting ~1100 lines of duplicated CSS and TSX in favor of one consistent component.
Tightened onboarding's create-task detection to only complete when the agent or project count exceeds the initial baseline, so users with pre-existing defaults aren't auto-credited on first launch.

entries.title: Z_BILLING_API_KEY plumbed into desktop nightly
entries.summary: Release Infrastructure + Desktop: the billing API key is now passed from CI into the desktop build and baked into the binary at compile time.

items.text: Add the Z_BILLING_API_KEY secret to the desktop nightly workflow and bake it into the desktop binary via build.rs so shipped desktop builds can authenticate against the billing service without runtime configuration.

highlights: Full light/dark theming with a Settings → Appearance editor, custom tokens, and JSON presets
First-run onboarding: welcome modal, 5-task checklist, and friendlier empty states
Light mode is now actually readable across chat, sidekick, modals, and form inputs
Two CEO-chat blanking bugs (LRU eviction and cold-load reveal) fixed
Aura3D remembers your last project and auto-selects the latest image/model
